business.headline: Will Sensex, Nifty recover tomorrow? 3 things stock market investors should knowDalal Street closed deep in the red as Gulf tensions and rising oil prices spooked investors, raising the key question of whether markets will recover tomorrow or stay under pressure.

G7 considers emergency oil reserve release amid Gulf crisisG7 finance ministers are scheduled to hold a call with Fatih Birol, executive director of the International Energy Agency (IEA), to discuss the impact of the ongoing war involving Iran on global oil markets.

Can crude oil rally trigger inflation in India? FM Nirmala Sitharaman explainsReplying in writing in Parliament, Nirmala Sitharaman said domestic price levels are currently near the lower end of the Reserve Bank of India's (RBI) tolerance band, which should help limit the impact of higher crude prices on inflation.

Sensex, Nifty sink to near 1-year lows as crude oil jumps on Iran warThe Nifty 50 index fell 1.73% to close at 24,028.05, its lowest closing level in 10 months. The market's volatility (VIX) also surged to a 21-month-high, showing that uncertainty among investors has increased.

Is the world ready for the 'unthinkable' amid West Asia conflict? IMF chief asksGeorgieva said shipping activity through the Strait of Hormuz has dropped by about 90%. The narrow waterway is one of the most important energy routes in the world.

How 'lucky number 7' helped unravel an alleged cement cartelThe issue surfaced during a 2018 tender by ONGC for a cement order. When the bids were opened, the company noticed that all competing firms had quoted exactly Rs 7,000 per tonne.

Using offshore gaming apps? Nithin Kamath warns of rising scam risksAfter restrictions were imposed on several real-money gaming platforms, many offshore betting apps have started appearing across social media and messaging platforms.

Crude oil prices cross $110 amid Gulf conflict. Will your pocket feel the pinch?While the government has said crude oil imports are continuing in "full flow" through alternative routes and that energy supplies remain secure for now, analysts warn that a prolonged spike in crude prices could still affect consumers if the rally continues.

Saudi Aramco sells oil on spot market amid war-led supply disruptionThe Iran war has led to the effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz, one of the world's key oil routes, disrupting shipments and supply flows.

3 IPOs opening this week: Rajputana Stainless, Innovision, Raajmarg Infra InvITThese IPO offerings span different sectors — stainless steel manufacturing, manpower services and road infrastructure, thereby giving investors varied choices depending on their risk appetite and investment interest.

Meesho shares slide 10% after Rs 1,500 crore tax demand for FY24. Details hereMeesho said it is currently reviewing the assessment order and does not agree with the observations made by the tax authority.
The company added that it has adequate legal and factual grounds to challenge the demand and will take the necessary steps to protect its interests.

SBI share price falls 6%: Should you sell or hold the PSU stock?The share price fall wiped out nearly Rs 62,352 crore in market capitalisation. With this drop, the bank's valuation slipped below the Rs 10 lakh crore mark to around Rs 9.93 lakh crore.

Rupee falls to record low amid crude oil shock: What it means for IndiaThe rupee dropped 0.6% to 92.3350 against the US dollar, breaking its previous record low of 92.3025 that was touched last week. Rupee has remained under pressure since the United States and Israel launched military action against Iran last week.

Stock market crash: IndiGo, SBI, Maruti among worst hit amid ongoing sell-offAmong the worst-hit stocks on the index were InterGlobe Aviation (IndiGo) and State Bank of India (SBI), reflecting the broad-based nature of the market decline.

Rupee sinks to all-time low of Rs 92.30 against US dollar as oil prices riseThe rupee slipped in early trade as investors turned cautious amid escalating geopolitical tensions in the Middle East and a sharp spike in global oil prices.

Sensex crashes 1,400 points: 3 reasons why stock market tumbled todayRising Middle East tensions, a sharp spike in crude oil prices and renewed foreign investor selling triggered a steep sell-off on Dalal Street, sending benchmark indices sharply lower in early trade.

Monday meltdown as Sensex tanks over 2,000 points over oil, Gulf crisisRising oil prices and escalating tensions in West Asia rattled investors, triggering heavy selling on Dalal Street as benchmark indices tumbled sharply, and most sectors traded deep in the red.

Sensex, Nifty opening: Stock market to crash today? Check trading setupAt around 8:25 am, GIFT Nifty futures were trading at 23,749. This signals a nearly 3% drop for the Nifty 50 from Friday's closing level of 24,450.45.

Why your bank keeps pushing insurance plans you may not needYou trust your bank for financial advice. But what if the person across the desk is under pressure to sell something else? This story examines how sales targets shape what bank relationship managers recommend.

Let's set the record straight: Women are better at managing money than menThe financial world has long been seen as a man's playground. Yet from household budgets to investment portfolios, women are steadily proving that patience, discipline and long-term thinking, not risky bets, are often the real keys to managing money well.

entertainment.headline: Kareena, Ananya Panday and Kalyani Priyadarshan on hypermasculine trend in films Actors Kareena Kapoor Khan, Kalyani Priyadarshan and Ananya Panday shared their views on the growing trend of hypermasculine male characters in Indian cinema.

What Vikram Bhatt posted about Vijay-Trisha amid wedding outing, divorce case Filmmaker Vikram Bhatt defended Vijay and Trisha after the two were spotted together at a wedding amid the actor-politician's divorce battle. In a long Instagram note, Bhatt said people should stop judging actors' personal lives.

Kamal Haasan reportedly highest-paid actor in Kalki 2898 AD. Can you guess his fee? Reports reveal actor Kamal Haasan received a record fee for his role in director Nag Ashwin's sci-fi epic Kalki 2898 AD. His friend and actor Yugi Sethu discussed the veteran actor's reported salary in a recent interview.

Mahesh Babu returns after Georgia schedule of SS Rajamouli's Varanasi: Reports Actor Mahesh Babu was reportedly seen at Hyderabad airport, returning from Georgia after wrapping up a schedule for Varanasi with filmmaker SS Rajamouli.

Daisy Shah hits back at X user questioning her Iftar party attendance Actor Daisy Shah addressed a social media comment that attempted to equate her opposition to firecrackers during Diwali with her participation in Iftar celebrations, emphasising that the two issues are unrelated.

Vishal Dadlani calls Arijit Singh a genius, says follow your heart Vishal Dadlani voiced support for Arijit Singh after the singer announced he is stepping away from playback singing. Vishal called Arijit a musical genius and encouraged him to follow what makes him happy.

Dhurandhar 2: Experts decode paid preview strategy behind Ranveer Singh's thriller Ranveer Singh's Dhurandhar: The Revenge, directed by Aditya Dhar, is generating strong buzz ahead of its release. Industry experts note that the paid preview trend has worked for several major films in the past, and the sequel is already seeing strong audience demand.

Manjummel Boys director Chidambaram gets bail in sexual harassment case The Ernakulam District and Sessions Court has granted anticipatory bail to Chidambaram, director of the film Manjummel Boys, who faces allegations of sexual harassment. The bail comes with strict conditions set by the court.

Shakira adds second Mumbai show after massive fan response. Check dates, venue Global music star Shakira will perform three times in India this April, adding a second Mumbai concert due to overwhelming interest and making her first major return to the country in nearly 19 years.

He is doing fine, need your prayers: YouTuber UK07 Rider Anurag Dobhal's wife UK07 Rider Anurag Dobhal's wife Ritika has shared a health update after the YouTuber's shocking car crash. The incident followed a livestream in which he called the drive his "final."

Saif Ali Khan on pay disparity: Not about gender, it's about who's filling theatres Saif Ali Khan shared his views on the pay disparity debate in the film industry, saying actors with equal stature should be paid the same.

Dhurandhar 2 on OTT: When and where to watch Aditya Dhar's thriller Aditya Dhar's spy thriller Dhurandhar 2, starring Ranveer Singh, is set for a theatrical release on March 19. As fans eagerly await the sequel, here's when the film is expected to arrive on OTT.

Explained: Why Trisha slammed director Parthiban amid Vijay-Sangeetha's divorce row Actor Trisha Krishnan firmly responded to director R Parthiban's dig at her at a recent award show. In this piece, we explain the context behind the controversy and what led to their publicly played-out confrontation.

Vijay's Jana Nayagan revising committee screening postponed: Report A scheduled screening for Vijay's Jana Nayagan by the revising committee of the Cental Board of Film Certification has reportedly been cancelled after a committee member became unwell. This has delayed the film's certification process indefinitely.

Priyanka Chopra's Women's Day post celebrates women who shaped her On International Women's Day, Priyanka Chopra shared a heartfelt tribute to the influential women in her life. In her post, Chopra also included a quote by Coco Chanel.

YRF postpones Alia Bhatt's Alpha for the second time, confirms theatrical release YRF's Alpha, starring Alia Bhatt and Sharvari, has been postponed yet again. The spy-universe film will now release in cinemas in the second half of the year.
Suga at 33: BTS' 'human cat' who turned honesty into art As BTS rapper-producer Suga turns 33, fans are celebrating the artist known for his honest lyrics and powerful music.

YouTuber UK07 Rider Anurag Dobhal remains in ICU after crash, wife visits hospital YouTuber Anurag Dobhal is in the ICU after crashing his car during a livestream watched by thousands. His manager has shared a health update as details about the disturbing "final drive" emerged, and revealed that Dobhal's wife visited him in hospital.

Dhurandhar The Revenge advance booking: Ranveer Singh's film on pre-sales rampage Ranveer Singh's Dhurandhar: The Revenge is already setting the box office ablaze with massive advance bookings. With over two lakh tickets sold in two days and Rs 18.11 crore collected, the film's pre-release momentum is hard to ignore.

general news.headline: LPG cylinder for Rs 1,500 as Iran war triggers panic buying. Is gas really scarce?The Iran war has seen Tehran choking the Strait of Hormuz, one of the busiest routes for oil and gas supplies. This has resulted in panic buying of LPG cylinders in parts of India. What do officials and experts say? Is there an LPG shortage in India?

WTF, miffed US told Israel after it hit 30 Iranian oil depotsIsrael's strikes on 30 Iranian fuel depots went far beyond what the US had anticipated when it was notified in advance, marking the first major disagreement between the two allies since the war began on February 28, Axios reported.

Rift in Iran leadership? What President's U-turn, Mojtaba's appointment revealAlthough Iran's leadership remains united in continuing the fight against the US and Israel, signs of division are emerging in Tehran. The backlash over President Masoud Pezeshkian's apology for attacks on neighbouring states, and the appointment of Mojtaba Khamenei as the supreme leader despite resistance, suggest growing discord.

UPSC clarifies after Rank 301 confusion, Ghazipur's Akanksha Singh's claim confirmedThe Union Public Service Commission has issued a clarification after reports surfaced that two aspirants with the same name, Akanksha Singh, were claiming Rank 301 in the Civil Services Examination 2025 results declared on March 6.

Gambhir responds to MS Dhoni's viral T20 World Cup tribute: Great seeing youT20 World Cup final: After MS Dhoni's glowing tribute, Gautam Gambhir responded back to his former captain and teammate after India's T20 World Cup win.

Live: Nato downs Iran missile over Mediterranean, Turkey warns against escalationUS-Israel Attacks Iran Live Updates: As the Middle East war entered its 10th day, Iran launched fresh attacks on Israel and several Gulf countries on Monday, hours after state television reported that Mojtaba Khamenei, son of the country's late supreme leader, had been named his successor. The escalation rattled global markets, sending oil prices sharply higher and sparking fears that rising energy costs could fuel inflation and curb consumer spending in the United States. Meanwhile, Saudi Arabia warned Tehran it would be the "biggest loser" if attacks on Arab states continued after a drone strike targeted its Shaybah oil field. Follow indiatoday.in for all latest updates on the Middle East crisis.

Why did India stick with Abhishek Sharma? Gautam Gambhir explains selection ideologyT20 World Cup: Gautam Gambhir explained why India continued backing Abhishek Sharma despite his lean run in the T20 World Cup. The gamble paid off as the young opener smashed a record-breaking fifty in the final.

Does India have low-cost killer kamikaze drones like Iran's Shahed?Low-cost drones are reshaping warfare, as seen in Ukraine for the last four years, and now in the Iran war. Iran's low-cost Shahed drones have inspired the US's LUCAS. In India, low-cost, deep-strike drone projects like Sheshnag-150 and Project KAL are picking pace.
Why Gen Z can afford a sabbatical at 27 but millennials often can'tAt 27, some workers are planning their first sabbatical. At 37, many are still paying off the life they built.

Two Lok Sabha MPs don't take salary. Here's who they areAn exclusive Right To Information response accessed by India Today reveals that only two MPs in the current Lok Sabha have chosen not to draw their salary. While one MP belongs to the BJP, the other is from the Congress.

Is your job in the red zone? The Anthropic AI chart shows which careers are changingThe debate around AI and jobs often swings between hype and alarm, but a new visual based on research by Anthropic brings a rare dose of clarity to the conversation. By comparing AI's theoretical capabilities with how workers are using it today, it sketches an early map of how the modern workplace is shifting. The question now is,  where do you stand on that map?

What is Kharg Island? Trump weighs strike on Iran's oil hub as Gulf war heats upAs the Gulf war intensifies, Kharg Island has emerged as a high-stakes flashpoint, with US President Donald Trump reportedly weighing options to strike Iran's oil hub — a move that could cripple Iran's economy but also send shockwaves through global energy markets.

What Vikram Bhatt posted about Vijay-Trisha amid wedding outing, divorce caseFilmmaker Vikram Bhatt defended Vijay and Trisha after the two were spotted together at a wedding amid the actor-politician's divorce battle. In a long Instagram note, Bhatt said people should stop judging actors' personal lives.

How Robotic Knee Replacement is Redefining the Journey Back to MobilityEarly consultation with an orthopaedic specialist is essential. Delaying surgery in severe cases can lead to worsening deformity and reduced functional outcomes.

How 'lucky number 7' helped unravel an alleged cement cartelThe issue surfaced during a 2018 tender by ONGC for a cement order. When the bids were opened, the company noticed that all competing firms had quoted exactly Rs 7,000 per tonne.

Wife, lover kill man, pass it off as heart attack. Remarriage exposes murder plotA woman murdered her husband with the help of her lover and tried to pass it off as death due to natural causes. Her remarriage exposed the murder plot.

Do elites 'hate' a mainstream sport?Pickleball had its moment. Now, padel is the new obsession. But behind the switch is there a bigger story about status, relevance, and exclusivity that we are missing?

US struck Iran school where 175, including girls, died? What we know so farA new video purportedly shows a Tomahawk missile hitting an Iranian base located next to a school where 175 people, most of them young girls, died on the opening day of the ongoing US-Iran war. The US is the only country in the world to operate the Tomahawk missile.

Manchester-bound IndiGo flight returns to Delhi due to airspace curbsAccording to flight tracking data, the aircraft reached close to the airspace near the border of Ethiopia before abruptly turning around mid-flight. The aircraft later re-entered Indian airspace.

Israel fired white phosphorus munitions over Lebanon homes, claims rights bodyThe Israeli military unlawfully used artillery-fired white phosphorus munitions over homes on March 3, 2026, in the southern Lebanese town of Yohmor, according to Human Rights Watch.

Max Alexander, 10, turns saree into couture at his Paris Fashion Week debut10-year-old dressmaker Max Alexander made fashion history with his Paris Fashion Week debut, presenting a collection crafted from recycled materials reimagined as couture.

India now has over 50 living CheetahsThe birth marks a proud moment for Project Cheetah, which aims to restore the world's fastest land animal to Indian landscapes decades after it went extinct in the country.

Israel allegedly fired white phosphorous over Lebanon: What is it?The injuries caused by white phosphorus are among the most horrifying documented in modern warfare, and they are almost impossible to treat.
The Big BOOM Theory: GOAT is dead, all hail the new kingGOAT ruled one timeline. But GOAT's run is over. Now meet BOOM: Best Of Our Multiverse.

sports.headline: Watch: Rohit Sharma bows down to Jasprit Bumrah's mother after T20 World Cup win Rohit Sharma met Jasprit Bumrah's mother after India's T20 World Cup 2026 win, honouring her support behind Bumrah's stellar performance. Bumrah won the Player of the Match award in India's 96-run win on Sunday against New Delhi.

India will aim for a white-ball treble: Atherton raises 2027 ODI World Cup hopes T20 World Cup 2026, IND vs NZ: Former England captain Michael Vaughan heaped praise on India after they won their third title after beating New Zealand by 96 runs in the final on Sunday in Ahmedabad.

Suryakumar Yadav finally responds on the viral petrol pump photo in 2014 Suryakumar Yadav explained the story behind two viral old photos, including one from a petrol pump inauguration and another 'Ahmedabad calling' post

Gautam Gambhir explains India's high-risk, high-reward formula after T20 World Cup win T20 World Cup 2026, IND vs NZ: Speaking exclusively to India Today's sister channel AajTak, Gambhir said that a team can't succeed in T20 cricket with a subdued approach. On Sunday, India won the tournament after beating New Zealand by 96 runs in the final in Ahmedabad.

Stay humble, smash 300: Gambhir sets sights on bigger goals after T20 World Cup win T20 World Cup 2026: Gautam Gambhir said India still has bigger goals after the T20 World Cup win, adding the team wants to push the limits of T20 batting and hopes to breach the 300-run mark in the future.

Why did India stick with Abhishek Sharma? Gautam Gambhir explains selection ideology T20 World Cup: Gautam Gambhir explained why India continued backing Abhishek Sharma despite his lean run in the T20 World Cup. The gamble paid off as the young opener smashed a record-breaking fifty in the final.

My trophy holding his: Sanju Samson's wife in awe of India batter after T20 World Cup heroics Sanju Samson's wife, Charulatha Remesh, expressed her admiration after his match-winning 89 in the T20 World Cup 2026 final against New Zealand, helping India defend the title.

Sanju Samson set to receive hero's welcome in Trivandrum after T20 World Cup triumph Sanju Samson is set to receive a hero's welcome in Thiruvananthapuram after starring in India's T20 World Cup triumph, with the Kerala government planning a special reception for the Player of the Tournament.

PV Sindhu pulls out of Swiss Open days after returning from chaos-hit Dubai PV Sindhu has pulled out of the Swiss Open after returning from a stressful three-day ordeal in Dubai. The travel disruption had earlier forced the two-time Olympic medallist to miss the All England Championships.

Sanju Samson's father: Made him follow only Sachin Tendulkar and Rahul Dravid T20 World Cup, IND vs NZ: Sanju Samson's father, Samson Viswanath, revealed that he trained his son from childhood by having him follow the batting styles of Sachin Tendulkar and Rahul Dravid.

How Abhishek Sharma's redemption had a touch of Dube magic in T20 World Cup final T20 World Cup: Abhishek Sharma revealed he used Shivam Dube's bat in the T20 World Cup final. The gamble worked as the opener smashed the fastest fifty in a T20 World Cup knockout, ending his run slump.

Gambhir responds to MS Dhoni's viral T20 World Cup tribute: Great seeing youT20 World Cup final: After MS Dhoni's glowing tribute, Gautam Gambhir responded back to his former captain and teammate after India's T20 World Cup win.

Mushfiqur Rahim congratulates India for T20 World Cup win, 10 years after infamous postBangladesh's Mushfiqur Rahim congratulated India on their T20 World Cup triumph, nearly a decade after facing backlash for mocking India's exit from the 2016 edition.

Sanju Samson is a combination of Virat Kohli and Rohit Sharma: KumbleT20 World Cup final: Anil Kumble said that Sanju Samson was a mix of Virat Kohli and Rohit Sharma after delivering a record-breaking performance for India at the ICC Men's T20 World Cup. Samson scored three consecutive fifties after returning to the playing XI and helping India win the title.

Ravi Shastri slammed after ruining India's T20 World Cup-winning moment on commentaryRavi Shastri called the final wicket of India's historic T20 World Cup win the ninth, not the tenth. The gaffe immediately went viral, overshadowing one of cricket's greatest moments.

Suryakumar Yadav just like Rohit Sharma, cool, calm and collected: Sunil GavaskarT20 World Cup: Sunil Gavaskar compared Suryakumar Yadav's calm and composed leadership to Rohit Sharma after India's T20 World Cup triumph. With the win, Suryakumar joined Dhoni, Rohit and Kapil Dev as World Cup-winning Indian captains.

We are unfortunate that Sanju Samson saved his best for T20 World Cup: SantnerT20 World Cup final: Mitchell Santner said that his side was unfortunate to face the wrath of Sanju Samson after having got the better of him during the T20I series against India earlier in the year. Samson scored 89 in the final to help India win the trophy.

India's 2026 T20 champions the best ever? Harbhajan shuts Rohit-Dhoni era comparisonsT20 World Cup: Harbhajan Singh urged fans not to compare India's 2026 T20 World Cup champions with past teams. The former spinner said every era is different, even as he praised the current side for taking T20 cricket to another level.

Another prediction backfires: Amir reacts angrily as India win T20 World CupT20 World Cup final: Mohammad Amir had an angry reaction when asked about getting his prediction wrong after India won the T20 World Cup 2026 title. Amir also criticised New Zealand for handing the trophy to India.

Not just God's plan: Gautam Gambhir credits Indian fans for T20 World Cup triumphIndia's head coach Gautam Gambhir dedicated the T20 World Cup title to 1.4 billion fans, calling it the plan of an entire nation after India's historic 96-run win over New Zealand in the final.

India have ruined cricket: Salty Shoaib Akhtar reacts to one-sided T20 World Cup winT20 World Cup: Shoaib Akhtar lashed out after India's dominant T20 World Cup triumph, claiming the team has "ruined cricket." The former Pakistan pacer compared India to a rich kid dominating neighbourhood games.

Are we living in India's era of dominance in ICC events? Coach Gambhir reactsT20 World Cup final: Gautam Gambhir refused to say that it is an era of dominance for India in ICC events after they lifted the T20 World Cup crown. It was India's second ICC title win in a row.

Watch: Gavaskar keeps T20 World Cup promise, dances with SuryakumarT20 World Cup final: Sunil Gavaskar kept his promise to Suryakumar Yadav and danced with the Indian captain after India retained their T20 World Cup crown in Ahmedabad. Gavaskar heaped praise on Suryakumar after the win.

Emotional Ishan Kishan dedicates T20 World Cup triumph to late cousin sisterT20 World Cup: Ishan Kishan revealed he lost his cousin sister in a car accident a day before the T20 World Cup final. The emotional wicketkeeper-batter dedicated India's historic triumph to her memory after the win.

In photos: Inside India's T20 World Cup victory celebrationT20 World Cup: India delivered a ruthless all-round performance in Ahmedabad, overpowering New Zealand to defend their T20 World Cup crown as Abhishek Sharma, Sanju Samson, and Jasprit Bumrah headlined a dominant night.

Why didn't Glenn Phillips bowl more in T20 World Cup final? Santner explainsT20 World Cup final: Mitchell Santner explained the reason why he used Glenn Phillips for one over during the T20 World Cup final against India in Ahmedabad. New Zealand also dropped Cole McConchie for the contest.

Three-time world champions, are India the greatest T20I side ever?T20 World Cup final: No men's team had successfully defended the T20 World Cup before this. India are now the only side to win three T20 World Cup titles, defend the trophy, and lift it on home soil. With achievements that no other team in the format can match, are India now staking its claim as the greatest T20I side ever?

The boy who stayed: Suryakumar Yadav's 25-year wait for a Sunday in the sunHe waited longer than most for the India cap, longer still for the spotlight. Now in Ahmedabad, Suryakumar Yadav stands as proof that sometimes the game's brightest stars bloom last. From the boy who lingered at the nets in Anushakti Nagar to a World Cup-winning captain, this is his story.

Fearless, ruthless, peerless: India make the World Cup final a one-team showT20 World Cup: India didn't win the World Cup final. They annexed it. From the first ball to the last, New Zealand were spectators to something they had no answer for.

After T20 World Cup win, India's next target is LA Olympics: SuryakumarLA Olympics in 2028 is India's next target, revealed India captain Suryakumar Yadav at the post-match press conference. The Indian cricket team is set to feature in the Olympics for the first time with the sport's return to competition.

Virat Kohli's special message to 'chetta' Samson after Player of the Tournament awardT20 World Cup final, IND vs NZ: Star India batter Virat Kohli wrote a special message for Sanju Samson after he won the Player of the Tournament award after India's historic T20 World Cup 2026 triumph, beating New Zealand by 96 runs in the final at the Narendra Modi Stadium in Ahmedabad.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
